source.description: Six-panel built-in image generation contact sheet saved under drafts/canoe-on-a-lake/ and cropped into raster tutorial assets.
source.notes: Subject chosen after a recent-lesson back-check; it uses a boat silhouette, calm water, and simple outdoor setting distinct from the recent tugboat lesson.

process_strategy: A single six-panel contact sheet keeps the canoe at the same side angle while each frame adds hull, rim, seats, paddle, lake ripples, and restrained shading before the final darkening pass.

final_elements.name: long canoe hull
final_elements.description: A narrow side-view canoe hull with lifted bow and stern.

final_elements.name: inside rim and seats
final_elements.description: A visible inner rim with two simple cross seats.

final_elements.name: resting paddle
final_elements.description: A slim paddle laid diagonally across the canoe.

final_elements.name: lake ripples
final_elements.description: Loose horizontal water marks placed under and around the canoe.

final_elements.name: soft shore and shadow
final_elements.description: A pale distant shore line and light contact shadow that support the canoe.

frames.visible_job: Blocks in the long shallow hull guide and light water baseline.
Turns the guide into a lifted bow, lifted stern, and curved lower hull.
Adds the inside rim line and two cross seats across the open canoe.
Places a diagonal paddle with a narrow handle and simple blade.
Adds water ripples, a pale shore line, and a soft shadow under the canoe.

final_step.visible_job: Only darkens keeper hull, rim, seat, paddle, ripple, shore, and shadow marks with restrained blue-brown pencil touches.
allowed_changes: small line cleanup on established elements

rejection_checks: No major contour, prop, marking, or color choice should first appear in the final image.
No adjacent frame should look unchanged at card size.
The final image must keep the same pose, scale, and placement as the step frames.
